capability_clusters.name: Media viewer / DAM
preserved_intent: Image-first portfolio viewer with global and per-character carousel/photo surfaces.
Full-image display, horizontal thumbnails, slideshow, fullscreen, filters, ratings, favorites, notes, tags, provenance, missing-media states, and repair workflows.
Media rows distinguish normal images, pending rows, deleted/archive rows, and OpenPose sidecars.
handshake_notes: Preserve behavior, but move product output paths away from .GOV and replace CKC labels.

capability_clusters.name: Character sheets
preserved_intent: Character sheet plus portfolio viewer is the core product shape.
User-authored text is byte-preserved with no rewriting, censorship, field hiding, or silent field loss.
Sheet parsing, structured block editing, protected public IDs, merge/versioning, templates, cloning, and bulk character operations are first-class.
handshake_notes: Replace CKC schema names with Handshake-native storage contracts and keep text preservation tests.

capability_clusters.name: Inbox / Intake
preserved_intent: Direct image ingress routes toward Intake.
Persistent batches, item selection, resume, linked/loose classification, pending Inbox parking, character/sheet/collection linkage, and explicit clipboard/URL import actions.
Pending lifecycle is safe and never silently deletes source files.
handshake_notes: Treat full batch sheet-field editing and per-batch Inbox folder UI as follow-up debt.

capability_clusters.name: Collections / contact sheets
preserved_intent: Collections are cross-character curated image sets with notes, tags, optional character/sheet-version links, slideshow, and export.
Contact sheets have SVG plus manifest provenance, deterministic no-space paths, source IDs/hashes, tags, layout metadata, and optional character/sheet-version links.
Raster contact sheet export remains planned.
handshake_notes: Use Handshake artifact roots and managers instead of CKC_GOV/targets paths.

capability_clusters.name: Sidecars / versioning
preserved_intent: Sheet versions are append-only and reverts create new versions.
OpenPose PNG/JSON artifacts are sidecars hidden from normal galleries unless requested.
Archive/restore, orphan manifests, event logs, entity revisions, compatibility fixtures, migration invariants, and idempotency checks preserve data safety.
handshake_notes: Port reset/orphan intent without CKC installer assumptions.

capability_clusters.name: PoseKit / OpenPose
preserved_intent: PoseKit workbench supports blank, single-photo, and collection contexts with durable rigs and session-scoped workspace tabs.
OpenPose output includes body-18, face-70, hand-left-21, hand-right-21, zero-filled hands when absent, yaw/pitch/roll head pose, and sidecar exports.
Identity profiles store deterministic crops, landmarks, measurements, pose metadata, and bridge payloads.
Blocked calibration/history interaction work remains explicit.
handshake_notes: Keep WP-0133 debt as a dedicated interaction pass before claiming parity.

capability_clusters.name: ComfyUI bridge
preserved_intent: Localhost-only intake/replay with token support, workflow history, prompt extraction, replay, stats, and registered outputs.
Workflow and Pose tabs expose replay paths.
Identity profile references can flow into IPAdapter or face-reference workflows.
Prompt-response matrix remains future intent.
handshake_notes: Rename CastKitCodexBridge and CKC API strings to Handshake-native names.

capability_clusters.name: Automation / debug / manual
preserved_intent: Model-facing automation exposes typed commands, state, sessions, heartbeats, leases, logs, and captures.
Background automation must not focus, foreground, use OS input, or steal attention.
In-app manual and command dispatcher must be consistency-tested.
UI WPs require normal and constrained visual captures plus console/runtime error reporting.
handshake_notes: Adapt to Handshake's runtime while keeping product code out of .GOV.

capability_clusters.name: Search / tags / similarity
preserved_intent: Global search spans sheets, docs, moodboards, images, tags, and provenance with grouped results and jump targets.
Smart folders, tag manager, links/backlinks, AI suggestions, palette search, duplicate detection, and perceptual similarity are power-user workflows.
Tag operations mutate structured tags only and never rewrite free text.
handshake_notes: Do not copy SQLite or FTS5 into Handshake in any form; port behavior to PostgreSQL/search storage.

capability_clusters.name: Exports
preserved_intent: Export hub covers empty templates, LLM packs, filled sheets, image sets, moodboards, collections, share packs, backups, web portfolios, contact sheets, and batch exports.
Exports support field/section selection and presets.
Generated names are no-space, portable, and provenance-preserving.
handshake_notes: Replace CKC default paths and D-drive rule with Handshake path-safety and artifact-root rules.

source_requirements.requirement: No censorship, no rewriting, no euphemizing; store and export user text byte-for-byte.
Adult and explicit fields are first-class and always enabled.
Template integrity gates prevent silent Field ID drops and preserve descriptor-on-same-line formatting.
Default experience is a two-panel image-first portfolio viewer beside the character sheet.
Media pane has non-overlay header controls, slideshow, controls panel, bottom metadata, empty filter state, horizontal thumbnails, keyboard navigation, fullscreen, and dropzone.
Active library and missing media are visible; repair by hash is dry-run by default and writes a report.
Internal character ID is stable storage key; public Character ID is system-managed and protected.
Enum fields accept free text while offering suggestions; suggestions are field-specific and explicit.
Sheet ingest/merge is selective, diffed, append-only, versioned, and protects CHAR-ID-001.
Folder-only Intake sorts into pass/reject/pending; linked mode imports accepted/pending and preserves rejected sources.
Persistent Intake batches support selection, linked/loose classification, character/sheet/collection linkage, pending Inbox parking, and OpenPose derivative recognition.
Collections carry notes, tags, optional character/sheet-version links, additive batch tag application, and contact-sheet creation.
Contact sheets are SVG plus manifest artifacts with source IDs/hashes, tags, layout metadata, and optional character/sheet-version linkage.
Normal gallery and collection flows hide deleted/archived rows and OpenPose sidecars unless explicitly requested.
PoseKit opens blank, single-photo, or collection workbench contexts and reports context/source/sidecar counts.
OpenPose exports include body, face, and hand arrays; hand arrays are canonical 63-float left/right arrays with zero-filled absent hands.
PoseKit head pose persists yaw/pitch/roll with quaternion-backed math and UI reset paths.
Identity profiles store deterministic face crop, landmarks, measurements, pose metadata, and bridge payload.
ComfyUI bridge supports localhost intake, history, prompt extraction, replay, stats, registered outputs, and replay from Workflow/Pose tabs.
Automation exposes typed state, manual, sessions, leases, logs, commands, renderer/backend dispatch, and captures.
Background automation must not show, focus, foreground, notify, flash, use global shortcuts, or use OS input APIs.
Manual, command map, dispatcher, and preload wiring must be consistency-tested.
Links and backlinks connect characters, docs, and images without silent auto-rewrite.
Tag manager renames/merges structured tags, shows counts, pins tags, and preserves free text byte-for-byte.
Global search spans sheets, notes, stories, moodboards, image metadata, with grouped results, snippets, and jump targets.
AI tag suggestions are optional, review/apply only, confidence-bearing, cancellable in bulk, and local-server compatible.
Visual similarity uses perceptual hashes or equivalent, thresholded results, and safe jump-to behavior.
Empty and filled character exports preserve canonical template bytes and support LLM-friendly field/section presets.
Backups use manifest and checksums, validate before restore, and require explicit restore confirmation.
Static web portfolio export produces offline HTML/CSS/JS and optimized/copy images with field/image scope controls.
Compatibility checks pin legacy fixtures, additive migrations, handler routing, template field IDs, idempotency, backup traceability, and indexes.
Parallel editing uses PostgreSQL source of truth, sessions/leases, optimistic revisions, append-only product events, and limited safe CRDT-style merges.

taskboard_status.meaning: Taskboard marks these items DONE. Preserve them as completed CKC source intent and validation shape, not as Handshake proof.
Taskboard marks these items REVIEW. Preserve implemented intent and useful evidence while requiring fresh Handshake verification.
PoseKit calibration, markers, and history are blocked on draggable calibration overlay, missing-marker placement flow, 3D/live split editing, and forked history.

Planned/concept/research intent for multi-subject scenes, Pose polish, shortcuts, detector research, prompt-response matrix, PostgreSQL-only testing, explicit SQLite rejection, and raster contact sheets.

Preserve test patterns: manual-command consistency, synthetic input invariants, background stealth, parser/validator/block editor, reset/orphan manifests, compatibility suites, PoseKit math/UI/live captures, PostgreSQL regression tests, tsc/build/full npm test evidence.

conflicts.ckc_assumption: SQLite tables, SQLite FTS5, triggers, better-sqlite3, and db/codex.db appear in older spec examples and fallback paths.
conflicts.handshake_reset_rule: Use PostgreSQL only. SQLite is not accepted in Handshake in runtime, tests, fixtures, mocks, examples, fallbacks, cache, compatibility adapters, temporary harnesses, imports, exports, or product paths.
conflicts.mitigation: Port behavior to PostgreSQL storage interfaces and require no-SQLite tripwires.

conflicts.ckc_assumption: Electron standalone app, portable exe, NSIS reset modes, app.getPath userData, CKC_main, and package scripts.
conflicts.handshake_reset_rule: Rebuild as Handshake product modules unless the operator explicitly chooses standalone packaging.
conflicts.mitigation: Separate product capabilities from packaging and create Handshake-native runtime roots.

conflicts.ckc_assumption: CastKit Codex, CKC, CKC_main, CKC_GOV, ckc-config.json, ckc IPC names, CastKitCodexBridge.
conflicts.handshake_reset_rule: Rename product-facing strings and namespaces to Handshake/Atelier Lens equivalents.
conflicts.mitigation: Run naming migration matrix before implementation.

conflicts.ckc_assumption: Dev automation captures and build outputs write under CKC_GOV/targets.
conflicts.handshake_reset_rule: Handshake product runtime must not read or write .GOV; build/test/tool outputs belong under external artifact roots.
conflicts.mitigation: Route captures, exports, caches, and reports to configured product runtime/artifact roots.

conflicts.ckc_assumption: Evidence references D:/ sample paths and old OpenRepose folders.
conflicts.handshake_reset_rule: New stored paths must be repo-relative, runtime-root-relative, or operator-configured.
conflicts.mitigation: Keep absolute paths as historical source evidence only.

conflicts.ckc_assumption: CKC installer reset modes use NSIS and CKC library layout.
conflicts.handshake_reset_rule: Reset behavior must be product-native, bounded, reversible where possible, and aligned with destructive-operation safety.
conflicts.mitigation: Model reset/orphan recovery as Handshake-native storage lifecycle features.

conflicts.ckc_assumption: Earlier CKC docs/gov mirrors existed in product docs before cleanup.
conflicts.handshake_reset_rule: Machine-readable governance contracts are authoritative; Markdown is projection/reference when typed contracts exist.
conflicts.mitigation: Avoid parallel manual JSON/Markdown sidecars as co-authority.

conflicts.ckc_assumption: FTS5 implementation details are embedded in spec examples.
conflicts.handshake_reset_rule: Preserve search behavior, not the SQLite/FTS5 implementation.
conflicts.mitigation: Use Handshake PostgreSQL/search-indexing architecture with equivalent result semantics.

preserved_goals: Rebuild CKC capability intent inside Handshake as a character portfolio, DAM, sheet, intake, collection, PoseKit, ComfyUI, automation, search, and export system.
Preserve user-authored content byte-for-byte and keep adult/explicit fields first-class.
Keep image-first workflows central across carousel, photos, metadata, tags, ratings, annotations, collections, contact sheets, and exports.
Make ingestion safe with explicit user actions, persistent batches, pending lifecycle, linked/loose modes, and no silent deletes.
Maintain identity and versioning through internal IDs, public IDs, sheet versions, sidecars, provenance, event logs, revisions, manifests, and recovery reports.
Support mass-parallel model/operator work through automation state, leases, manuals, captures, debug surfaces, and command consistency tests.
Integrate PoseKit/OpenPose with image collections, workflow history, identity profiles, and ComfyUI replay.
Make search, tags, similarity, palette, duplicate detection, and batch operations power-user-grade.
Provide exports for humans, LLMs, sharing, backups, web portfolios, contact sheets, and production handoffs.
Replace CKC-specific standalone assumptions with Handshake-native product modules, storage boundaries, and artifact hygiene.

proposed_wp_mapping.title: Core Library, Character, Sheet, Media, And Governance-Compatible Storage
proposed_wp_mapping.goal: Establish the Handshake-native data model and UI foundation for the image-first character portfolio, sheet editor, DAM viewer, versioning, tags, ratings, metadata, docs/moodboards, and recoverable media behavior.

microtask_buckets.outline: Storage contract for characters, images, sheets, docs, moodboards, tags, ratings, provenance, versions, and revisions
Product data root, export root, cache root, and external artifact root without .GOV writes
Internal and public character ID model
Sheet ingest, merge, diff, apply, and append-only version UI
Tag manager and saved searches baseline
Character templates, cloning, and batch operations

proposed_wp_mapping.title: Intake, Collections, Sidecars, PoseKit, OpenPose, And ComfyUI Production Workflows
proposed_wp_mapping.goal: Rebuild the production workflow layer that moves images from intake to curated collections, contact sheets, PoseKit rigs, OpenPose sidecars, identity profiles, workflow replay, and ComfyUI bridge outputs.

proposed_wp_mapping.title: Automation, Manual, Search, Similarity, Export, Backup, Parallel Editing, And Hardening
proposed_wp_mapping.goal: Add the model-facing operation layer, global search/tag/similarity intelligence, export/backup surfaces, parallel-editing controls, compatibility tests, and verification gates required for Handshake-scale rebuild quality.
